content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
December 8, 1997
Mary Herr
City Clerk
City of Mill Valley
Post Office Box 1029
Mill Valley, California 94942
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. 1-97-547
Dear Ms. Herr:
This letter is in response to your request for informal assistance regarding the provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTION
May surplus funds be donated to the city’s general fund wherein candidates for city
council reside and ran for office, or must they be donated exclusively to the State General Fund?
CONCLUSION
A candidate for city council may donate any surplus campaign funds to the general fund
of the city where the candidate ran for elective office.
ANALYSIS
You inquire about the term “General Fund” that is contained in Section 89519, which was
amended by Proposition 208. Section 89519(b) states that any remaining surplus funds shall be
distributed to any political party, returned to contributors on a pro rata basis, or turned over to the
1 Government Code Sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, Sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity
provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)
428 J Street, Suite 450, Sacramento, California 95814 (916) 322-5660


File No. 1-97-547
Page 2
General Fund.
2 The Commission, at its November 1997 meeting, considered a regulation
interpreting the surplus funds statute.
3 It indicated that it prefers an interpretation in which the
term “General Fund” means the general fund of the jurisdiction in which the official ran for
elective office.
4
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
rJnsJ
1
By:
Lynda Doherty
Political Reform Consultant, Legal Division
SGC:LD:tls
2 In addition, Section 85313(e) states that any funds in an officeholder account remaining after leaving
office shall be turned over to the General Fund.
Regulation 18519.3 will be presented to the Commission for adoption at the January 15, 1998, meeting.
A copy ofthe notice is enclosed.
To the extent that this advice contradicts all or a portion of the advice contained in the Avila Advice
Letter, No. A-96-355, Jones Advice Letter, No. 97-084, and Tabachnik Advice Letter, No. 1-97-410, that portion of
the advice is hereby superseded.
1:\Advice Ltrs\97547.wpd

CONCLUSION: A candidate for city council may donate any surplus campaign funds to the general fund
of the city where the candidate ran for elective office.
